purpose: Quantifies the feasible stock of office-to-residential conversion through successive engineering and economic filters
components: Floor plate depth filter: >65ft eliminates 50-60% of stock
Structural/MEP feasibility filter: eliminates 30-40% of remainder
Zoning and regulatory filter: eliminates 20-30% of what passes physical filters
Economic viability filter: costs of $150-400/sqft require acquisition below $100-200/sqft
current_assessment: Total addressable: $20-50B of ~$3.5T office asset class or less than 1.5%. Conversion is irrelevant to the 2025-2027 maturity wall. Self-referential dynamic: conversion only becomes viable at prices below current marks
limitations: Assumes current building codes and zoning which could change with policy intervention
Cost estimates may vary significantly by market
Does not account for potential technological innovations in conversion methodology
